methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante, a Series E climate and energy company founded by Claude Letourneau, has raised $318M to date. The valuation universe spans 21 same-sector peers, including 8 private companies (TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape among them) and 1 public comparable (LNZA). However, a narrow peer-median approach is not applicable here: while 21 Climate & Energy companies exist in the dataset, none yielded three or more datapoints in critical filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables), making a sector median unreliable.

Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($318M) as a baseline and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement, the analysis implies $445M. This single-method triangulation carries material limitation: without sufficient peer anchors, the step-up assumption cannot be cross-validated against market transactions.

The analysis supports a median valuation of $445M, with a range of $378M (low-end stress, applying 1.2x multiplier) to $512M (high-end case, applying 1.6x multiplier). Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set comparables and reliance on a single methodology. A follow-on valuation would benefit from additional transaction data in frontier energy or expanded same-stage peer coverage to reduce methodological concentration risk.
